description: Validates the runShell `shell` field permutations: the built-in bash default (bash-only syntax must pass on every OS, exercising the lazy Git Bash resolution on Windows), an explicit `shell: bash`, the Windows-only `cmd` and `powershell` shells (runOn-gated so they SKIP elsewhere), pipes through bash's coreutils on Windows, and runCode's bash interpreter on Windows (the historical unsupported-on-Windows guard is lifted). The cmd/powershell-off-Windows FAIL guard and the config-level `shell` default can't be expressed as PASS/SKIPPED fixtures; they're asserted in test/core-core.test.js and test/shell-resolution.test.js.

tests.description: No shell field: defaults to bash on every platform. `[[ ]]` is a bashism that errors under cmd.exe and under dash, so this passing proves the default is genuinely bash.
Explicit `shell: bash` behaves identically to the default, on every platform.
Pipes through bash's coreutils work on Windows (grep ships with Git Bash / MinGit, and the runner puts the bash toolbox on PATH).
`shell: cmd` runs through cmd.exe on Windows: %VAR% expansion and the `ver` builtin only exist there. SKIPPED on macOS/Linux via runOn.
`shell: powershell` runs through Windows PowerShell: $PSEdition is Desktop there and unexpanded anywhere else. SKIPPED on macOS/Linux via runOn.

Pipes and nested double quotes through cmd.exe's `/d /s /c` invocation — coverage for the cmd-specific spawn plumbing that the bash default no longer exercises implicitly. SKIPPED on macOS/Linux via runOn.
A background process started through the bash shell honors the resolved shell in the background spawn path (bash-only $(( )) syntax in the readiness output), then stops via closeSurface.
A background process started through the cmd shell honors the resolved shell in the background spawn path (%VAR% expansion only happens in cmd). SKIPPED on macOS/Linux via runOn.
runCode language bash on Windows resolves the same lazily-installed Git Bash (the old hard FAIL is lifted). POSIX coverage for runCode bash lives in runCode.spec.json.
